_comment: WC2026 Group L, MD3. Standings before: England 4, Ghana 4 (1-0 Panama, 0-0 England), Croatia 3 (2-4 England, 1-0 Panama), Panama 0. This match DECIDES 2nd: Ghana (4 pts) qualify 2nd with a draw; Croatia (3 pts) MUST WIN to overtake them (a draw leaves Croatia 3rd on 4 pts, still in best-third contention). So Croatia has a legitimate STRUCTURAL incentive to attack (must win), while Ghana -- a proven stingy block that held England 0-0 and has conceded 0 all group -- will be content to sit deep and protect the point. That is exactly the organized-underdog archetype (England-Ghana 0-0, the same Ghana): Croatia the quality favourite, but Ghana's discipline keeps the draw/Under a live tail. Croatia (Modric-led) the better side and goal-hungry by necessity. Home = Croatia, away = Ghana. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).
_thirds_context: Best-thirds table cross-check (top 8 of 12 qualify; FIFA tiebreak pts -> GD -> GF). As it stands Croatia would be the 7th-best third (3 pts, GD -1, GF 3) -> IN, but J/K/L MD3 are still pending so the cutline will rise. Croatia's real scenarios: WIN -> 2nd, direct (and Ghana becomes a safe 4-pt third); DRAW -> 3rd on 4 pts, which today ranks top-4 among thirds -> almost certainly IN; LOSE -> stuck on 3 pts with GD -2 or worse -> borderline 8th-9th, dependent on how the J/K thirds finish, and GD becomes the knife-edge. Net: Croatia only needs to AVOID DEFEAT to be safe, which reinforces (not weakens) its incentive to push and protects the attacking lift. Ghana, on 4 pts, is virtually through no matter what: win/draw = 2nd, and even a LOSS leaves them a safe 4-pt third -> they defend the point pragmatically, not out of desperation.

meta.stakes: Decides 2nd place: Ghana (4 pts) go through with a draw; Croatia (3 pts) must win to take 2nd (a draw leaves them 3rd, best-third contention). Per the thirds table Croatia needs only to avoid defeat to qualify (a draw = a safe 4-pt third); Ghana is virtually through in every outcome. Croatia the quality side with the attacking incentive; Ghana a stingy block protecting the point.

home.name: Croatia
notes: Quality, Modric-led; scored 2 vs England. MUST WIN -> attacks (legitimate structural incentive, not a motivation guess). attack 1.22, context 1.05.
Decent but not airtight at the back (conceded 4 to England). defense 0.95.

away.name: Ghana
notes: Stingy, organized: 0-0 England, conceded 0 all group. attack 0.85 (low, plays for the point).
Hard to break down -> keeps the draw/Under live. defense 0.92.
A draw qualifies them 2nd -> will sit deep and protect it.

_postmatch.result: Croatia 2-1 Ghana. Croatia win and take 2nd (6 pts, GD 0), qualifying directly; Ghana finish 3rd on 4 pts (GD 0) and go through as one of the best thirds -- exactly the thirds-table scenario.
_postmatch.verdict: 1X2 hit and, more importantly, the STRUCTURAL read was vindicated: Croatia needed the win for 2nd, came out attacking, and got it. The lambdas were excellent -- Croatia 1.52 -> 2 goals, Ghana 0.86 -> 1 -- so the xG calibration was spot on. The thirds cross-check also paid off: Croatia winning -> 2nd direct, and Ghana losing -> a safe 4-pt third, precisely as flagged. The only texture miss was the goals envelope: I leaned Under 2.5 (57.5%) and BTTS No (54.3%), but it was 2-1 (Over + BTTS Yes). Ghana's block leaked a goal -- and since Ghana were already virtually qualified (a loss still made them a safe third), they had no need to lock down 0-0, which fits the slightly more open game.
_postmatch.lesson: STRUCTURAL incentive (must-win for 2nd + GD safety) correctly drove the attacking lift and the side call. Refinement on texture: when the underdog block is ALREADY safe (Ghana qualified even in defeat), it has less reason to sit ultra-deep -> don't over-short BTTS/Over the way you would against a desperate, must-not-concede bus. Stakes shape game-state, and a comfortable block plays more openly than a cornered one.
